summary: Plan has 4 convergent BLOCKING issues identified by all four reviewers from independent angles. The plan's primary structural premise — a class-based ComposerTool hierarchy with REGISTERED_TOOLS/ARGUMENT_MODEL/RESPONSE_MODEL — does not exist in the codebase. tools.py (5481 lines) uses six function-pointer dispatch dicts and no base class. The runtime walker promised by spec §4.2 omits recursion into list[BaseModel] and dict[str, BaseModel], creating a Tier-1 audit-corruption path that the adequacy guard cannot detect. The spec at revision 4 contains the same false premise (§4.4.1 line 649) and requires amendment. Rewriting the plan from scratch is recommended over in-place patching: the precondition gap is structural, not a localized error.

convergence_signal.note: Four-of-four convergence on the structural precondition issue is signal that the plan is not patchable in place. Two-of-two convergence on the recursion gap is signal that the spec walker contract and the runtime implementation diverged before plan authoring.

blocking_issues.issue: Class-based ComposerTool hierarchy does not exist; entire plan precondition is fictional
blocking_issues.evidence: tools.py lines 5250-5314 define six function-pointer dispatch dicts (_DISCOVERY_TOOLS, _MUTATION_TOOLS, _BLOB_DISCOVERY_TOOLS, _BLOB_MUTATION_TOOLS, _SECRET_DISCOVERY_TOOLS, _SECRET_MUTATION_TOOLS). No class ComposerTool, no REGISTERED_TOOLS registry, no ARGUMENT_MODEL/RESPONSE_MODEL on any class. Every tool is a plain function taking args: dict[str, Any]. Tasks 6, 8, 9, 10, 11 all assume the hierarchy.
blocking_issues.reversibility: Pre-merge: Easy (1). Post-merge: Difficult (2) — would require reshaping ~30 tools.
blocking_issues.resolution: Either (Path A) scope a pre-Phase-2 class-conversion sub-phase explicitly with its own tracer-bullet, or (Path B) redesign Phase 2 around function-keyed manifest dispatch (tool_name → redaction declaration). Path B preserves Sensitive[T] for tools that already use Pydantic internally without forcing a ~30-tool refactor. Recommendation: Path B unless the operator wants the class-hierarchy refactor for independent reasons.

blocking_issues.issue: Runtime walker apply_sensitive_redaction does not recurse into list[BaseModel] or dict[str, BaseModel]; adequacy guard cannot detect the gap
blocking_issues.evidence: Spec §4.2 lines 442-462 explicitly promises descent into nested BaseModel containers. Plan Task 6 walker implementation omits recursion into list/dict element types. walk_adequacy reads ARGUMENT_MODEL/RESPONSE_MODEL (which don't exist; see B1) so the gate is doubly broken. Result: every Sensitive[T] field nested inside a list-of-models or dict-of-models leaks through with no detection signal. CI green, audit corrupted.
blocking_issues.reversibility: Difficult (2) — audit data corruption is hard to reverse once persisted
blocking_issues.resolution: (1) Co-define the walker contract and adequacy guard so they share acceptance criteria; future schema changes must touch both or neither. (2) Add explicit test coverage for list[BaseModel], dict[str, BaseModel], Optional[BaseModel], Union[BaseModel, ...], and tuple[BaseModel, ...]. (3) Use Pydantic's model_fields traversal rather than hand-rolled introspection.

blocking_issues.issue: Spec §4.4.1 line 649 falsely claims tools declare arguments via Pydantic BaseModel subclasses
blocking_issues.evidence: Spec at revision 4 contains the same false premise the plan inherits. Plan rewrite alone is insufficient — spec must be amended to revision 5 to remove the BaseModel-declaration claim and re-derive Phase 2 design from the actual function-pointer architecture.
blocking_issues.resolution: Spec revision 5: rewrite §4.4.1 to describe the actual tools.py architecture; re-derive §4.2 walker contract and §4.4 redaction-policy attachment mechanism. Coordinate with plan rewrite — spec amendment is a hard prerequisite to any Phase 2 plan.

blocking_issues.issue: Task 13 unconditionally invokes gh pr create, violating operator PR-confirmation discipline
blocking_issues.evidence: ELSPETH operator memory: PR-open is explicitly deferred and operator-confirmed (see Phase 1B and 1C memory entries: 'PR-open deferred'). Task 13 Step 4 schedules gh pr create unconditionally.
blocking_issues.severity: Medium (2) — process discipline, not data integrity
blocking_issues.resolution: Remove gh pr create from Task 13. End plan at 'gate green; await operator PR-open instruction' as Phase 1B/1C did.

warnings.issue: ComposerTool name collides with existing ComposerToolInvocation/ComposerToolStatus/ComposerToolRecorder L0 family
warnings.recommendation: If class hierarchy is adopted (Path A), rename to ComposerToolBase or ComposerDispatchTool.

warnings.issue: Calendar synchronicity at migration: all tools migrated same day → 365-day mass batch expiry → operator bumps dates without review
warnings.recommendation: Stagger initial last_reviewed_iso values; or replace date-based review with content-hash-based review (review-required when redaction policy changes, not on calendar).

warnings.issue: MissingToolError for LLM-hallucinated tool name treats Tier-3 input as crash condition
warnings.recommendation: Unrecognized tool name from LLM → ARG_ERROR audit record + continue (Tier-3 quarantine). Reserve crash semantics for CI registry-consistency check.

warnings.issue: Telemetry call uses untyped duck typing (.summarizer_errors_total etc)
warnings.recommendation: Define Protocol with the required attributes; type-checker enforcement.

warnings.issue: Summarizer non-string return value silently flows into Tier-1 audit row
warnings.recommendation: Crash on non-str return per CLAUDE.md tier model; do not coerce.

warnings.issue: len(repr(value)) discloses structural metadata (RSK-03 weak echo)
warnings.recommendation: Remove length disclosure; emit fixed-form '[REDACTED]' marker only.

warnings.issue: Mass-copy uniqueness check (spec §4.2.2) absent from plan
warnings.recommendation: Add explicit assertion that no two tools' why_arguments_safe summaries exact-match.

warnings.issue: Sensitive[T] vs ToolRedactionPolicy precedence undefined when both declared
warnings.recommendation: Define precedence in spec; add test.

warnings.issue: @elspeth/security CODEOWNERS team likely does not exist; CODEOWNERS as primary security control is theatre
warnings.recommendation: Either create the team or remove the CODEOWNERS gate. Defense in depth ≠ defense by paperwork.

warnings.issue: Task 9 under-scoped to a single heading covering ~30 tools across 3 signature shapes
warnings.recommendation: Split Task 9 into: standard tools (13), blob tools (9, extended sigs), secret tools (3, extended sigs).
Replace with direct typed attribute access per CLAUDE.md offensive-programming policy.

warnings.issue: Snapshot covers only legacy-policy tools; new tools added between bootstrap and review have a false-pass window
warnings.recommendation: Snapshot bootstrap must enumerate all tools at creation; CI must fail when a tool exists but has no snapshot entry.

minor_and_nit_issues.issue: sorted() on non-string keys may error at runtime
minor_and_nit_issues.recommendation: Coerce keys to str before sort, or assert key type.
Parse once, pass parsed structure to redactor.
Note expected runtime cost in plan; benchmark at 30+ tools.

must_fix_before_any_code_lands: Decide Path A (class-conversion sub-phase) or Path B (manifest dispatch) — operator decision required
Spec revision 5 amending §4.4.1 and re-deriving Phase 2 design from actual tools.py architecture
Plan rewrite (not in-place amendment) — precondition gap is structural
Co-defined walker + adequacy-guard contract covering list/dict/Optional/Union/tuple of BaseModel
must_fix_before_merge: Remove unconditional gh pr create from Task 13
Replace MissingToolError crash with Tier-3 ARG_ERROR audit-and-continue for LLM hallucinations
should_fix_before_merge: Stagger initial last_reviewed_iso values to avoid calendar synchronicity
Define telemetry Protocol with typed attributes
Split Task 9 into 3 sub-tasks by signature shape
Replace hasattr/getattr default forms with direct typed access
Bootstrap snapshot for all tools (not just legacy-policy)

conflicts_resolved.issue: None — reviewers converge with no contradictions
conflicts_resolved.note: All four reports point to the same structural defect (B1) from different observation surfaces (symbol existence, architectural shape, test-coverage gap, system-dynamics feedback). Quality and systems converge on B2 from runtime-correctness vs feedback-loop angles respectively. No reviewer recommends a course of action that contradicts another reviewer's recommendation.

remediation_path_recommendation.recommended: Rewrite plan from scratch after spec revision 5 lands
remediation_path_recommendation.rationale: The precondition gap (B1) is structural. A class hierarchy that does not exist cannot be patched into the plan; the plan must be re-authored against the actual function-pointer dispatch architecture. Doing this in-place would require touching every task and would not surface the design choice between Path A and Path B for operator decision. A clean rewrite forces that decision into the open.
ordering: 1. Operator decides Path A vs Path B
2. Spec revision 5: amend §4.4.1, re-derive §4.2 walker contract and §4.4 redaction-policy attachment based on chosen path
4. New plan-review cycle on the rewritten plan
remediation_path_recommendation.spec_amendment_cost: Spec is at revision 4. Revision 5 must amend §4.4.1 (false BaseModel-declaration claim) and re-derive §4.2 (walker contract) and §4.4 (policy attachment) from the actual architecture. Estimate: a substantial rewrite of three sections, not a localized fix. The cost is unavoidable — the false premise is the derivation root for the entire Phase 2 design.
